# DESYNC FIX-LIST — frontier_dag.json (138 desyncs)

**Date:** 2026-07-04 · **Status:** PROPOSALS ONLY — no registry/atlas/status edits made; owner to bless.
**Source read:** `03_THEORY_MAP/frontier_dag.json` "desyncs" array (generated by `tools/build_frontier_dag.py`
from registry `09_LEAN_FORMALIZATION/docs/CLAIM_TO_LEAN_MAP.csv` + 12 `04_VERIFICATION/*BLOCKERS*.csv`
+ 2 boards + 3 no-go atlas MDs). Every desync was re-derived and cross-checked against its source file.
**Edit discipline:** all fixes target sources (registry notes / atlas CSVs / boards / builder), then regen
`frontier_dag.json`. Never edit the generated JSON.

## Stats (all 138 accounted)

| Class | Issue string | Count | Reduced to |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | "in atlas, absent from registry notes" | 34 edges | 34 candidate notes-mentions — but only ~14 are semantically real; 20 are TOTAL_CLOSURE_BOARD **cross-product artifacts** (lane rows bundle k prims × m owners) |
| 2 | "atlas endpoint not in registry (atlas:…)" | 56 edges | **43 unique missing D0 ids** (planned-chain rows) + **4 free-text endpoints** (builder schema artifact, single root cause) |
| 3 | "lean_status=OPEN but release_status=CERT-CLOSED" | 1 | D0-CVFT-F3 — **judged LEGITIMATE, not inflation** (§Class 3) |
| 4 | "in registry notes, absent from all atlases" | 47 edges | registry is canonical → atlas/board lag; 15 primitive groupings, incl. 9 primitives missing from the catalog |

**Suggested order:** F-01, F-02, F-09 (kill the two systematic generators + close the false positive) →
F-03..F-06, F-10..F-13 (cheap verified one-liners, ~15 desyncs) → F-07/F-08 + F-16..F-19 (chain policy,
~40 desyncs) → F-14, F-15, F-20 (hygiene). After each batch: rerun `tools/build_frontier_dag.py` and
diff the desync count.

---

## Class 3 verdict — D0-CVFT-F3: LEGITIMATE (not status inflation)

- Cert exists and **passes when run** (`05_CERTS/vp_cvft_baryon_s3_scaffold.py`: "All algebraic checks
  passed for the finite S3 scaffold. No physical masses, no PDG, no external data used.").
- BOOK_05 §05.6 vocabulary: `CERT-CLOSED` = "Executable finite certificates verifying frozen core
  invariants" — python-cert closure; it does not assert Lean closure. `lean_status=OPEN` is the honest
  record that no Lean module exists yet.
- Notes AGREE with the field: scaffold certified (27D triple carrier, S3 rep, 10D symmetric carrier,
  U_eff^B admissibility); "spin/flavour transfer PDG and masses remain open"; scope tag
  `[was:OPERATOR-SCAFFOLD-CERTIFIED]` preserved.
- Contrast the SPECTRAL-EINSTEIN precedent: there the notes narrative ("Demoted CERT-CLOSED →
  PROOF-TARGET, cert cleared") contradicted the field — that is inflation-shaped. Here nothing
  contradicts; the cert-scope is stated in the notes themselves.
- Registry-wide rescan: D0-CVFT-F3 is the **only** lean_status=OPEN row with a closed release_status —
  no hidden second candidate.
- Proposed fix: **none to the row.** Optional builder refinement (F-02): suppress the flag when
  python_cert is non-empty AND the cert is on the cert-pass board (risk: could mask a future
  cleared-cert case — hence the board condition).

Fix legend: RENAME = correct atlas id to registered claim; REGISTER = add PROOF-TARGET registry row
(or apply the F-08 planning-only policy); DECIDE = owner call between the two.

"Perron >= 2|E|/N = 718/33 > phi^3…", "block-swap diag(P4,P3) vs diag(P3,P4)…"). Single root cause:
missing claim_id column + builder fallback. R5 produced no desync only because its primitive cell is
"(external carrier)".

## Method note

Counts re-derived directly from the JSON (34+56+1+47 = 138) and each class re-verified against its
source: class-1 provenance recomputed by replaying the builder's gate extraction over the boards and
no-go atlases; class-2 endpoints checked against all 538 registry claim_ids with near-match scan;
class-3 cert executed; class-4 primitives checked against the 11-row primitive catalog. All fixes are
proposals; nothing was edited outside this file and J2_HIGGS_NONCOMMUTE_CHECK.md.
